winner: Why did the tomato join the dance competition? Because it wanted to show off its new blend-of-the-night shoes!

candidates.text: Why don't shoes ever get lost at a paint party? Because they always blend in with the crowd!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative pun on 'blend in' with a play on paint party context; concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did the pair of shoes join the smoothie shop? Because it wanted to blend in with the fruit juice crowd!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Creative use of 'blend' and 'shoes,' concise and surprising twist with natural language."}

candidates.text: Why did the tomato join the dance competition? Because it wanted to show off its new blend-of-the-night shoes!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses both required words but relies on a somewhat predictable pun ('blend-of-the-night shoes') and lacks originality in the setup."}

candidates.text: A man walks into a bar and orders a drink. "Could you blend my shoes with this?" he asks the bartender, holding up his worn-out footwear. The bartender looks confused but obliges, blending the shoes in the blender before pouring it into a glass. The man takes a sip and smiles. "It's not bad, but I was thinking more of a shoe-smoothie.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses the required words 'blend' and 'shoes' in a creative way, but the humor is somewhat forced and relies on a niche pun. The setup is clear, but the punchline lacks the sharp surprise and specificity that would make it more effective."}

candidates.text: Why did the shoe go to the blender? It wanted to become smooth sole-dier!
